definition: The probability of a wage-price spiral is low (<10-15%) under current conditions. Only 2 of ~16 post-war cycles produced true spirals, and current conditions satisfy 0 of 3-4 conjunctive prerequisites: union density, COLA coverage, sustained wage growth >5%, and expectations de-anchoring.
evidence: 2021-23 natural experiment: 2/3 conditions temporarily met, no spiral materialized — genuine out-of-sample validation
Structural decline in union density and COLA coverage is secular, not cyclical
open_questions: Could novel labor market structures create spiral risk through channels not captured by the historical prerequisite set?
